report.summary: Built 2 learn guides: Ethereum Verkle Trees & Hegota Upgrade Guide (H2 2026 stateless clients) and Sui Hashi Bitcoin DeFi Guide (new BTC lending/yield protocol on Sui). Both pages added to PUBLISHED_PAGES.

sections.title: Research Brief
items: Ethereum Hegota upgrade (H2 2026) will ship Verkle Trees — replacing Merkle Patricia Tries with vector commitments, reducing node storage by 90% and enabling stateless clients that sync in minutes instead of hours
Verkle Trees use Inner Product Arguments (IPA) for polynomial commitments — no trusted setup required. Key EIPs: EIP-6800 (state in Verkle format), EIP-2935 (historical block hashes)
Sui Hashi protocol launched on devnet March 19, 2026 — institutional-grade Bitcoin DeFi bringing native BTC lending, borrowing, and yield without wrappers
Hashi backed by BitGo, Bullish, FalconX, Ledger, and Cubist — targets the <0.5% of Bitcoin ($1T+) currently used in DeFi
Hashi uses MPC custody + Sui smart contracts for collateral management; mainnet planned later 2026

sections.title: Content Created
items: Ethereum Verkle Trees & Hegota Guide 2026 (~3,200 words): Covers Verkle tree data structure, vector commitments, stateless clients, Hegota timeline, impact on validators/L2s, risks, and practical implications

Sui Hashi Bitcoin DeFi Guide (~3,000 words): Covers Hashi protocol architecture, MPC custody, key features (lending/borrowing/yield/bonds), Why Sui, institutional partners, BTCFi comparison table, risks, and timeline

sections.title: SEO Optimization
items: Verkle Trees page — Title: 'Ethereum Verkle Trees & Hegota Guide 2026 — Stateless Clients Explained | degen0x', Target keywords: 'verkle trees ethereum', 'ethereum hegota upgrade', 'stateless ethereum clients'. Schemas: Article + FAQ (6 questions)

Sui Hashi page — Title: 'Sui Hashi Bitcoin DeFi Guide 2026 — BTC Lending & Yield on Sui | degen0x', Target keywords: 'sui hashi protocol', 'bitcoin defi 2026', 'hashi bitcoin lending'. Schemas: Article + FAQ (6 questions)
Both pages include OpenGraph article tags, Twitter cards, structured data (Article + FAQ schemas), internal links to 5+ related degen0x pages, and disclaimers
Both pages added to PUBLISHED_PAGES in published-pages.ts (batch 17)
